description: Experimental scope insufficient for the central conjecture. All 42 AdamW experiments use a single architecture (ResNet-20, ~270K parameters) on two small datasets. The conjecture cannot distinguish between 'WD modulation never matters under AdamW' and 'WD modulation does not matter at CIFAR scale.' No ImageNet/ResNet-50, no VGG-16-BN, no ViT.
suggestion: Add VGG-16-BN on CIFAR-10/100 (42 runs) and single-seed ResNet-50 ImageNet with 3-4 key methods.

description: Two of four Phi modulation axes (spatial: AlphaDecay; target-norm: AdamWN) appear in Table 1 but have zero experimental coverage. The paper cannot make empirical claims about spatial or target-norm modulation.
suggestion: Add AdamWN to the experiment suite. Alternatively, explicitly narrow all claims to temporal and directional modulation.

description: Statistical power critically insufficient for a null-result paper. N=3 seeds, MDE ~0.7%, TOST confirming equivalence for 1/12 AdamW comparisons at delta=+/-0.5%.
suggestion: Increase seeds to N=5 for 5 critical comparisons. Pre-register TOST with delta=+/-0.3% margin.

description: Mechanistic hypothesis has empirical support but lacks formal analysis. No formal proof exists even in a simplified quadratic setting.
suggestion: Prove absorption mechanism in simplified quadratic loss setting under AdamW.

description: CIFAR-100 SGD experiments incomplete: random_mask 2 seeds, no_wd 1 seed. The 84-run benchmark claim is overstated.
suggestion: Complete missing CIFAR-100 SGD seeds (3 additional experiments). Update Table 5 with complete CIFAR-100 SGD results.

description: Appendix B and Appendix C referenced in paper body but do not exist.
BEM for half_lambda requires manual correction footnote. Pipeline computes 0.000 but table reports 0.500.
suggestion: Re-implement half_lambda with phi=0.5 so BEM pipeline computes correctly.

description: CWD paper's own random-mask ablation reportedly found CWD outperforms random_mask, contradicting this paper's finding. Discrepancy not addressed.
suggestion: Explain discrepancy: CWD was evaluated with Lion/Muon not AdamW. Make this explicit in Section 6.1/6.2.

description: AIS threshold interpretation inconsistency: all methods exceed AIS>0.2 threshold including random_mask, yet conclusion is 'alignment not exploitable.' CWD AIS (0.368) vs random_mask AIS (0.359) delta=0.009 never explicitly compared.
suggestion: Clarify threshold meaning and explicitly compare CWD AIS vs random_mask AIS in Section 5.3.
